recentNews.headline: Standard Intelligence raises $75M Series A from Sequoia and Spark Capital to build efficient computer-use models
recentNews.whyItMatters: Validates a ~6-person team at a reported ~$500M post-money, signaling intense investor appetite for video-trained computer-use foundation models versus screenshot/API-based agent approaches.

recentNews.headline: A Brief Fundraising Update: $75M from Sequoia and Spark, with angels Karpathy, Druckenmiller and Kovac
recentNews.whyItMatters: Company-confirmed round naming Sequoia's Sonya Huang and Spark's Yasmin Razavi plus marquee angels (Andrej Karpathy, Stanley Druckenmiller, Tesla Optimus lead Milan Kovac) — a strong syndicate-quality signal for a pre-revenue lab.

recentNews.headline: Standard Intelligence Rides Neolab Fervor with Computer Use Model
recentNews.whyItMatters: The Information frames Standard as part of a new wave of well-funded 'neolab' AI startups and reports its FDM-1 model being road-tested (a RAV4 with a dashboard laptop) in San Francisco, underscoring real-world ambition beyond benchmarks.

recentNews.headline: Standard Intelligence unveils FDM-1, the first fully general computer action model trained on ~11M hours of video
recentNews.whyItMatters: FDM-1 is the company's flagship technical differentiator (an encoder ~50–100x more token-efficient than prior SOTA), the asset that anchored the subsequent Series A raise.

recentNews.headline: FDM-1 autonomously drives a car through San Francisco after under one hour of fine-tuning
recentNews.whyItMatters: Demonstrates strong transfer learning from the video-pretrained foundation model to a physical control task — the headline proof point that the architecture generalizes far beyond on-screen software use.

recentNews.headline: Standard Intelligence open-sources hertz-dev, the first base model for full-duplex conversational audio
recentNews.whyItMatters: The team's earliest public credibility marker (an 8.5B Apache-2.0 audio model running ~120ms real-world latency on a single RTX 4090) established the research pedigree that later attracted Sequoia and Spark.
